params.text:
How quickly RIDDOR reports must be made

Timescales matter under RIDDOR. In general:

  • Deaths and specified injuries, dangerous occurrences and reportable diseases must be notified without delay (for the most serious cases, such as a death, by the quickest means — usually a phone call), with the formal report submitted within 10 days.
  • Over-seven-day injuries must be reported within 15 days of the accident.
  • Occupational diseases are reported as soon as the responsible person receives a written diagnosis.

Records of reportable events must be kept for at least three years. Reports are made through the HSE’s online reporting system.

params.alt: Reviewing incidents to learn and improve

params.question: A care worker injures their back while assisting a move and is unable to carry out their normal duties for nine days. Which RIDDOR category applies?

answers.text: An over-seven-day injury, reportable to the HSE within 15 days.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. Because the worker could not do their normal work for more than seven consecutive days, it is reportable within 15 days.

answers.text: A specified injury that must be reported within 10 days.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Not quite. A simple back strain is not a listed specified injury; the over-seven-day rule applies here.
No. Dangerous occurrences are specified near-miss events, such as equipment failure, not an individual’s injury.

answers.text: Nothing needs reporting because it is only a strain.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Incorrect. More than seven days off normal duties makes it a reportable over-seven-day injury.
